codeReview.summary: The handoff references commit 191c22c which is titled 'test: user testing validation for audit-trail milestone' and only contains audit-trail evidence files. The actual end-to-end validation work for cross-area flows (VAL-CROSS-001 through VAL-CROSS-005) was performed by the worker session based on transcript evidence, but the validation evidence was never committed to the polish milestone. The handoff commit reference is incorrect - 191c22c is an audit-trail milestone commit, not a polish/end-to-end-validation commit.

issues.description: Handoff references wrong commit: The handoff for end-to-end-validation (polish milestone) references commitId '191c22c' which is titled 'test: user testing validation for audit-trail milestone' and adds files to .factory/validation/audit-trail/ and evidence/audit-trail/. This commit does not contain any polish milestone or cross-area validation evidence.

Evidence not in commit: The transcript shows validation work was performed (browser automation for VAL-CROSS-001, database inspection for VAL-CROSS-004, code analysis for VAL-CROSS-005), but commit 191c22c only contains 3 audit-trail evidence files. No evidence for VAL-CROSS-001 through VAL-CROSS-005 is present in this commit.
VAL-CROSS-002 and VAL-CROSS-003 were validated in document-management milestone, not here. Cross-area reassignment and document lifecycle were tested separately in document-management user-testing.

Concurrent holds test: The feature verificationSteps mentions 'concurrent holds: one winner one loser' test, but the handoff doesn't explicitly confirm this scenario was tested. Capacity tests cover capacity conflicts but not true concurrent hold race conditions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Commit reference mismatch: The handoff for 'end-to-end-validation' in the 'polish' milestone references commit 191c22c, which is clearly an audit-trail milestone commit (title: 'test: user testing validation for audit-trail milestone'). The actual polish milestone scrutiny validation is in commit 3b1ef17, but even that commit references the wrong commit 191c22c.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show 191c22c --stat shows only .factory/validation/audit-trail/ and evidence/audit-trail/ files

sharedStateObservations.observation: Validation evidence committed to wrong milestone: The worker performed validation work (browser automation, capacity tests) but the evidence was committed to audit-trail (191c22c) instead of polish. This appears to be a documentation error in the handoff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows validation commands executed but commit only contains audit-trail files

sharedStateObservations.observation: Browser automation used appropriately: The worker used agent-browser skill for citizen OTP-to-confirmation flow (VAL-CROSS-001) which is the correct approach for end-to-end UI testing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff interactiveChecks show 'Browser automation: OTP login flow' and 'Browser automation: Citizen scheduling flow'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Code analysis used appropriately for expiration logic: Worker verified VAL-CROSS-005 (hold expiration) via code analysis of expireStaleCitizenHolds() rather than time-based live testing. This is reasonable for expiration verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff: 'Confirmed VAL-CROSS-005 (hold expiration) via code analysis'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Maildev not running during validation: Multiple transcript entries show 'ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:1025' errors when running tests, indicating Maildev was not started before tests. This is documented in the synthesis as a non-blocking infrastructure issue.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification.commandsRun shows test command with exitCode 1, and synthesis shows 'connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:1025'

summary: FAIL - The handoff references commit 191c22c which is an audit-trail milestone commit, not a polish/end-to-end-validation commit. While the transcript shows validation work was performed (lint, typecheck, browser automation, capacity tests), the actual commit only contains audit-trail evidence files. The cross-area validation evidence for VAL-CROSS-001 through VAL-CROSS-005 is NOT in this commit. The worker performed the work but evidence was committed to the wrong milestone. Recommend: either the handoff needs to reference the correct commit containing polish validation evidence, or the actual polish cross-area validation evidence needs to be committed.
